channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Texas AG sues New York doctor who allegedly prescribed abortion pills to woman in Lone Star State
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has filed a lawsuit accusing a New York doctor of prescribing abortion drugs to a Texas woman, violating laws in the Lone Star State.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Journalists anticipate a renewed hostility toward their work under the incoming Trump administration
item.description: When Donald Trump takes office as president for a second time, many journalists anticipate a hostile climate toward them and their work.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden, Harris thank major Democratic donors and urge them to stay engaged after tough loss to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden and Vice President Kamala Harris on Sunday thanked deep-pocketed Democratic donors who raised record sums in last month's election loss to President-elect Donald Trump and urged them not to lose hope and to remain politically engaged.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2024 was big for bitcoin. States could see a crypto policy blitz in 2025 in spite of the risks
item.description: The new year will usher in the bitcoin-friendly administration of President-elect Donald Trump and an expanding lobbying effort in statehouses.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump nominees should ‘steer clear’ of undermining polio vaccine, McConnell says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Republican leader Mitch McConnell, who had polio as a child, says any of President-elect Donald Trump’s nominees seeking Senate confirmation should “steer clear” of efforts to discredit the polio vaccine.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's tariffs threat is dividing business owners across the country
item.description: Businesses are divided over Trump's plan to impose sweeping tariffs. Some companies welcome the protection from foreign competition, while others worry about rising costs and retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley, MPR News

item.title: Trump appoints longtime foreign policy adviser Richard Grenell to serve as special missions envoy
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has picked longtime foreign policy adviser Richard Grenell to serve as an envoy for special missions.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: ABC agrees to give $15 million to Donald Trump's presidential library to settle defamation lawsuit
item.description: ABC News has agreed to pay $15 million toward Donald Trump’s presidential library to settle a defamation lawsuit over anchor George Stephanopoulos' inaccurate on-air assertion that the president-elect had been found civilly liable for raping writer E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pelosi has hip replacement surgery at a US military hospital in Germany after a fall
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi had hip replacement surgery Saturday at a U.S. military hospital in Germany after falling while at an event in Luxembourg with other members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 ways congressional investigations could change in the next Congress and beyond
item.description: The future of congressional investigations could be defined by Republican eagerness to probe private industry and less willingness to defer to the Justice Department, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Momentum builds around Social Security reforms ahead of Senate vote
item.description: Momentum is building around legislation that could mean higher Social Security benefits for some Americans as Senate leadership tees up a vote for next week.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats need new playbook to confront Trump, Kamala Harris pollster tells party
item.description: A top pollster to Vice President Kamala Harris says the Democrats need a new playbook to politically confront Donald Trump as he returns to the White House, and that they should focus on high prices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Manchin: Biden 'went completely insane on me' over American Rescue Plan opposition
item.description: “I was going to kill the whole bill. Then the president went completely insane on me,” Manchin said in an interview with news outlet Semafor that was published on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Nancy Pelosi hospitalized after she 'sustained an injury' from fall on official trip to Luxembourg
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi has been hospitalized after she “sustained an injury” during an official engagement in Luxembourg, according to a spokesman.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US military flies American released from Syrian prison to Jordan, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has transported out of Syria an American who had disappeared seven months ago into former President Bashar Assad’s notorious prison system and was among the thousands released this week by rebels, U.S. officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Frey issues another veto, rejecting council decision to deny raises for high paid city staff
item.description: Mayor Jacob Frey issued his third veto of the month Thursday, objecting to the Minneapolis City Council’s decision to withhold raises from city employees as a means of reducing the property tax levy increase.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Trump posts 'how it's going' message contrasting Time Person of the Year cover with mugshot
item.description: President-elect Trump posted a tongue-in-cheek "how it started, how it's going" post on his Truth Social network Friday after he was named Time's Person of the Year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants to turn the clock on daylight saving time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump wants to turn the lights out on daylight saving time. In a post on his social media site Friday, Trump said his party would try to end the practice when he returns to office.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 things to know about Biden’s 1,500 clemencies, pardons
item.description: President Biden pardoned 39 people and commuted the sentences of nearly 1,500 others on Thursday, a sweeping move that comes after pressure from criminal justice advocates over the surprise pardon of his son Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey drone sightings: Lawmakers press for more briefings
item.description: "Law enforcement and other agencies continue to support New Jersey and investigate the reports," the FBI said.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: A nonprofit leader, a social worker: Here are the stories of the people on Biden's clemency list
item.description: A nonprofit leader who supports at-risk New Orleans youth. A social worker who fosters animals. A counselor and recovery coach who volunteers at organizations that fight sexual assault and domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amazon to contribute $1 million to Trump’s inauguration fund. Meta is also donating $1M
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Amazon is planning to donate $1 million to President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration fund, a move that comes as major tech companies seek to improve their relationship with the incoming president.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz says he’ll propose anti-fraud measures, reflects on VP bid
item.description: In his first interview with MPR News since his vice presidential run, Tim Walz said he still has “privilege” of serving as Minnesota governor, urged lawmakers to work together in divided government.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: READ: Here is Biden's full list of clemencies
item.description: President Biden early Thursday announced that he is pardoning 39 people and commuting the sentences of nearly 1,500 others, setting a new daily record for clemency with a focus on those who were under home confinement during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Balluck, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Jacob Frey vetoes 2025 budget approved by Minneapolis City Council
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey announced Wednesday afternoon that he is vetoing the 2025 budget approved by the city council late Tuesday night. The budget had passed with enough votes to override a veto.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News
